channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Westpac reports modest profit drop amid Middle East crisis and rising energy costs
item.description: Westpac, Australia's second-largest bank, has reported a slight decline in its half-year profit, with net cash profit down 1 per cent to $3.483 billion for the six months ending 31 March, compared to ...

item.title: Kerala Assembly Election 2026: Key Winners and Ministers Who Lost
item.description: In the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections, the United Democratic Front (UDF) gained a significant lead over the Left Democratic Front (LDF) led by the Communist Party of India (Marxist). Prominent Congres...

item.title: Enugu lawmaker resigns from ADC to follow Peter Obi to NDC
item.description: Harrison Ogara wey dey represent Igbo-Eze South Constituency for Enugu State House of Assembly don officially resign from the African Democratic Congress (ADC). Dis move follow just hours after Peter ...

item.title: Manchester United Set to Earn Big After Securing Champions League Spot
item.description: Manchester United have officially secured their place in next season’s Champions League group stage after a thrilling 3-2 victory over Liverpool at Old Trafford. With this win, combined with Brighton’...

item.title: Eddie Butita Supports Bien’s Song Abroad by Paying Street Vendor in China
item.description: Comedian and scriptwriter Eddie Butita recently left Kenyans entertained after sharing a funny moment from his trip to China. While there, he paid a street vendor to play the popular song 'Finale' by ...

item.title: Pakistan Faces Growing Energy Crisis Amid Global Oil Supply Disruptions
item.description: With the Strait of Hormuz in a tense deadlock, global energy markets have entered an extremely uncertain period. Last month, the International Energy Agency forecasted slow growth in both oil supply a...

item.title: Alex Eala to face Magdalena Frech in Italian Open first-round match
item.description: Filipino tennis prodigy Alex Eala is set to compete against Poland's Magdalena Frech in the opening round of the Italian Open. This match marks an important step in Eala's professional journey, as she...

item.title: Saudi Arabia urges U.S. and Iran to ease tensions and resume talks amid Hormuz crisis
item.description: Saudi Arabia has warned of the risks of further regional escalation in the Persian Gulf, calling on the United States and Iran to de‑escalate and return to diplomatic negotiations. Riyadh is particula...

item.title: Discussion on Conspiracy Theories Surrounding Trump Dinner Shooting
item.description: The article examines why certain groups of people have speculated that the shooting incident at a dinner involving former President Donald Trump may have been staged. While details are limited, it hig...

item.title: High Production Costs Making It Tough for Kenyan Filmmakers to Create Quality Series and Films
item.description: Making films and TV series in Kenya is truly a labour of love but the expenses involved are making it very difficult for local creators to keep going. According to industry veterans like producer Okel...

item.title: Regis Resources and Vault Minerals to Merge, Forming Third-Largest ASX Gold Producer
item.description: Regis Resources and Vault Minerals have announced a merger that will establish one of the largest gold producers listed on the ASX, with an estimated combined value of nearly $11 billion. The new enti...

item.title: Teenager dies after being struck by a falling tree while playing in park
item.description: A 15-year-old boy, Brodan Dubickas, tragically died after being struck by a falling tree in Holmcroft Road, Stafford, while playing with friends. The incident occurred on Saturday evening, and despite...

item.title: Imaan Mazari-Hazir and Hadi Ali Chattha Sentenced: A Reflection on Justice and Fear
item.description: Imaan Mazari-Hazir and Hadi Ali Chattha, a married couple and human rights lawyers, have been sentenced to 17 years in prison over their social media posts, sparking outrage and concern across Pakista...

item.title: Fatal Collision Between Nile Star Bus and Trailer on Pakwach–Karuma Road Leaves Multiple Dead
item.description: In the early hours of Monday, May 4, tragedy struck along the Pakwach–Karuma Road when a Nile Star bus travelling from Arua to Kampala collided with a heavy trailer. The bus, registered UBP 736 U and ...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte says ICC case against former President Duterte will proceed to trial
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte has responded to recent decisions by the International Criminal Court (ICC) regarding her father, former President Rodrigo Duterte. During an interview, she confirmed that ...

item.title: South Africa and Nigeria Strengthen Ties After Eastern Cape Traditional Dispute

item.title: Chinese Coast Guard Monitors Filipino Civilian Ship in West Philippine Sea
item.description: A Chinese coast guard vessel was reported to have closely followed a Filipino civilian mission ship in the West Philippine Sea. The Filipino vessel, identified as MV Kapitan Felix Oca, is part of a ci...

item.title: Kenyan Man Shows Forgiveness by Saving Uncle Despite Past Conflicts
item.description: Brian Olucho’s story is a powerful example of forgiveness and compassion. For years, he faced challenges caused not by strangers, but by his own uncle, his father’s eldest brother, who allegedly sabot...
